sources.title: The War of the Worlds by H. G. Wells
sources.rightsBasis: Underlying work is public domain in the UK; Project Gutenberg plain-text edition; only short attributed excerpts are retained.

evidence.locator: Book One, Chapter V; the Heat-Ray attacks
evidence.anchor: It was sweeping round swiftly and steadily, this flaming death, this invisible, inevitable sword of heat.

evidence.locator: Book One, Chapter X; the narrator takes his wife away
evidence.anchor: We got to Leatherhead without misadventure about nine

evidence.locator: Book One, Chapter XIII; the narrator meets the curate
evidence.anchor: I became aware of him as a seated figure in soot-smudged shirt sleeves

evidence.locator: Book One, Chapter XVI; London flees
evidence.anchor: It was the beginning of the rout of civilisation, of the massacre of mankind.

evidence.locator: Book One, Chapter XVII; the warship intervenes
evidence.anchor: coming to the rescue of the threatened shipping.

evidence.locator: Book Two, Chapter IV; the narrator silences the curate
evidence.anchor: I turned the blade back and struck him with the butt. He went headlong forward and lay stretched on the ground.

evidence.locator: Book One, Chapter I; the Martian minds
evidence.anchor: intellects vast and cool and unsympathetic
evidence.requiredAnswer: intellects vast and cool and unsympathetic

evidence.locator: Book One, Chapter I; Ogilvy’s estimate
evidence.anchor: The chances against anything manlike on Mars are a million to one
evidence.requiredAnswer: The chances against anything manlike on Mars are a million to one

evidence.locator: Book Two, Chapter VII; the artilleryman’s judgement
Book One, Chapter VI; the Heat-Ray
evidence.anchor: It is still a matter of wonder how the Martians are able to slay men so swiftly and so silently.
evidence.requiredAnswer: It is still a matter of wonder how the Martians are able to slay men so swiftly and so silently.

evidence.locator: Book One, Chapter X; the narrator decides to leave
Book One, Chapter XVI; London’s flight
evidence.anchor: the rout of civilisation, of the massacre of mankind
evidence.requiredAnswer: the rout of civilisation, of the massacre of mankind
